title: papaver croceum subsp. corydalifolium
intro: Papaver croceum subsp. corydalifolium, also known as Himalayan poppy or Corydalis, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its alkaloid content, particularly protopine, which is also found in other Corydalis species. The medicinal properties of Papaver croceum subsp. corydalifolium have been found to provide relief from pain, anxiety, and stress, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant is commonly prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support respiratory health, improve sleep quality, and reduce inflammation.
